content.claim: Poland's 1999 reform extending single-structure basic schooling from 8 years, introducing a 6+3+3 system with a comprehensive middle tier (gimnazjum), was followed by PISA gains that placed Poland in the top 10 in reading and science and top 15 in mathematics, with particularly large improvements concentrated among students who previously would have entered vocational tracks
content.context: Fresh live research through the Polish system reform lens
limitations: Multiple confounding factors accompanied the 1999 reform (broader investment in education, demographic changes)
Causation cannot be definitively isolated to the structural change alone
Secondary source synthesis notes caution that attribution requires careful control of other variables
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
